items.title: Underwater gardening helping to restore Tasmania's lost kelp forests

children.content: A new live-in internship program on the Tasman Peninsula is working to repopulate the area's endangered giant kelp forests through underwater gardening.

gallery.altText: Video of dappled sunlight shining through giant underwater kelp forest

thumbnail.altText: Video of dappled sunlight shining through giant underwater kelp forest

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: 'Right fit': Ken Hinkley announced as inaugural Tasmania Devils coach

children.content: The former Port Adelaide coach is formally announced as the inaugural coach of the Tasmania Devils. It completes a year-long hunt for a senior coach for the AFL's newest club.

image.altText: A man leans against a wooden fence post

items.title: Minister insists Tasmania won't lose health services under sale to Catholic provider

children.content: Doctors are concerned Tasmanians will be forced to travel interstate for certain procedures if the Hobart Private Hospital is sold to Catholic provider Calvary, but the health minister insists there will be no service gap.

image.altText: Exterior signage at Hobart Private Hospital.

items.title: 'Sit down!' Joyce's dog interrupts 7.30 interview as he defends Hanson

children.content: The One Nation MP says Pauline Hanson's meeting with far-right British activist Tommy Robinson did not amount to an endorsement of his criminal conduct, rejecting suggestions she had crossed a "red line".
The otherwise serious 7.30 interview was twice interrupted by Mr Joyce's dog, prompting him to yell at it to "sit down".

items.title: How authorities failed to heed one woman's reports of WA's worst child rapist

children.content: Maggie Dawkins's efforts to shine a light on the horrific abuses committed by Dennis McKenna against a generation of boys in Katanning are set down in a new book.

image.altText: Dennis McKenna in brown shirt, tie and jacket on the left. On the right a photo of brick buildings.

items.title: 'Happening to everybody': AI-manipulated images exploited by scammers

children.content: Papua New Guinea prepares new laws to criminalise the malicious use of artificial intelligence, including deepfakes, voice cloning and digital impersonation.

image.altText: An AI-generated image showing Jennifer Baing with a group of people holding cash standing behind another pile of cash.

items.title: Six years out from hosting the Olympics, this is where Brisbane stands

children.content: Broken promises and scarcely broken ground. Five years since Brisbane secured the 2032 Olympic Games, this is the state of play.

gallery.altText: An artist's render of the Moreton Bay indoor sports centre.
a mound of dirt in a field which is set to be the moreton bay indoor sports centre

thumbnail.altText: An artist's render of the Moreton Bay indoor sports centre.
a mound of dirt in a field which is set to be the moreton bay indoor sports centre

items.title: US to impose 50pc tariffs on most Canadian goods

children.content: The White House says the tariffs will go into effect in 30 days, meaning there is time for negotiations.

image.altText: A bottleshop is shown with a sign saying 'we are no longer selling liquor from the USA' on a shelf of red wine bottles

items.title: ASX falls as oil prices near one-month high on US-Iran escalation

children.content: The Australian share market has fallen after a negative session on Wall Street. Oil prices remain near a one-month high as Yemen's Houthis threaten to impose a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia. See how the trading day unfolds on our blog. Follow live.

image.altText: A man watches the information screens at the ASX

items.title: US, Iran trade fire as Trump says Iran will 'pay' for soldiers' deaths

children.content: The United States has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran after identifying two soldiers killed while defending against Iranian missile and drone attacks in Jordan.

image.altText: A young female soldier in army greens in front of a US flag.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis declare maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia

children.content: In a statement, the Houthis' military spokesperson Yahya Saree says the group will enforce the blockade against its "criminal Saudi enemy", effective immediately.

image.altText: Yahya Sarea stands in a military uniform speaking at a podium.

items.title: Quiz: How well do you remember these Australian kids' TV shows?

children.content: What was the name of the tough, mangy cat in The Ferals? And which show featured EC, the faceless doll?

gallery.altText: Round The Twist cast in front of the lighthouse.

thumbnail.altText: Round The Twist cast in front of the lighthouse.

items.title: Why 'narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home

children.content: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work. Why? The study concluded "it interferes with their motivations for power and status".

image.altText: a man gesturing as he sits in front of a laptop

items.title: The discipline behind royal wedding cellist's success

children.content: Sheku Kanneh-Mason went viral performing at Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding. He's captivated audiences since and is touring Australia this month.

image.altText: Sheku Kanneh-Mason sits holding a gleaming cello, one hand reaching towards a music stand. There's a grand piano in the room

items.title: Firewood side hustle sparks multi-million-dollar business in four states

children.content: When bushfire and drought robbed farmer Peter Chauncy of his livelihood, he started chopping wood to sell. Forty years later, he runs one of Australia's largest firewood businesses.

gallery.altText: Man with grey hair holding firewood, wearing orange high-vis jacket, pile of firewood behind him
yellow bags of firewood sitting in front of fuel bowser

thumbnail.altText: Man with grey hair holding firewood, wearing orange high-vis jacket, pile of firewood behind him
yellow bags of firewood sitting in front of fuel bowser

items.title: Prepare for stock market pain as China throws a spanner into AI boom

image.altText: People cheer on balcony under SpaceX sign

children.content: Elon Musk's latest venture sent global stock markets to dizzying heights, but its performance since does not augur well for the company or the future of the AI boom.

items.title: Broncos winger Josiah Karapani handed $350 fine for drink-driving offence

children.content: Brisbane Broncos winger Josiah Karapani avoids a criminal conviction after pleading guilty to a drink-driving offence.

image.altText: A man in a white shirt flanked by journalists with mics

items.title: The four sets of data that show how the property market is transforming

children.content: Global instability, shifting tax policies and interest rate pressures force buyers and sellers to adjust to the changing property landscape.

image.altText: Houses under construction on a bright, sunny day

items.title: Tiny 'desert' fish found only in Australia narrowly avoids extinction

children.content: With scoops made from milk bottles, volunteers have been working tirelessly to save the red-fin blue-eye, found nowhere else in the world, from extinction.

gallery.altText: man holding up clear container with small fish in it

thumbnail.altText: man holding up clear container with small fish in it

items.title: Union boss accused of 'bullying' by Fire Rescue Victoria commissioner

children.content: Victoria's fire rescue commissioner has written a strongly worded letter to the secretary of the United Firefighters Union, accusing him of aggressive and inappropriate communication amid a long-running conflict.

image.altText: Peter Marshall wears a navy and yellow

items.title: SA government to explore legal options against developer

children.content: The SA government says it is exploring all legal options to respond to developer Wel.Co after it walked away from 75 contracts at a site north of Adelaide.

image.altText: A man in a dark blazer and blue shirt

items.title: Stubborn stigma fuelling shocking violence against sex workers in Australia

children.content: A new inquiry into violence against sex workers in Australia and Thailand comes just weeks after the murder of Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla, allegedly by an Australian man.

gallery.altText: a woman carries a sign saying 'stop mythologising men who kill sex workers'
A frame of a young Thai girl looking at the camera.

items.title: Tate brothers face court for first time since Miami arrest

children.content: Andrew and Tristan Tate's attorney says an attempt to extradite the brothers following their arrest in Miami is politically motivated.

image.altText: Tristan and Andrew Tate pictured sitting down in the audience at an event with serious expressions

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes British PM, provides AUKUS hints in first speech

children.content: Andy Burnham has pledged to provide immediate cost-of-living relief for Brits and invited US President Donald Trump to Manchester in the hours after becoming UK PM on Monday.

image.altText: A man in a suit speaking.

items.title: 'Surreal' sense of grief at packed town meeting after alleged Layla Jeffery murder

children.content: Victoria Police and the Buloke Shire Council have held an event updating 300 Donald community members on the alleged murder of Layla Jeffery.

items.title: Protests kick off ahead of NT child protection laws debate

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament is set to debate controversial child protection laws, which have been criticised for their potential impact on the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle.

image.altText: A sign saying 'every removal leaves a scar'

items.title: Shared lifestyle might be clue to why some couples have higher dementia risk

children.content: Having a spouse with dementia may put you at a higher risk of developing the condition, a new study finds.

image.altText: Elderly couple holding hands

items.title: PM backs bid for future NT AFL team, no promises on funding

items.title: Aussie world champs name new rugby sevens coach for LA Olympics

children.content: Rugby Australia makes a key appointment, as the world champion women's rugby sevens team looks ahead to the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

image.altText: Australia celebrates winning Bordeaux leg of rugby sevens World Championship.

items.title: 'I was in tears': Living without a heater through Canberra's winter

children.content: Barbara Belmonte, an ACT public housing resident, has been waiting weeks for her heater to be repaired amid the sub-zero temperatures of a Canberra winter — and she is not the only one.

image.altText: Barbara Belmont, public housing resident, sits and gestures while talking.

items.title: Moscow targeted by drones while Russia bombards Kyiv

children.content: Ukraine launches its latest attack on the Russian capital, firing more than 400 drones, while the Kremlin deploys more ballistic missiles against targets across Ukraine.

items.title: Football world 'devastated' by death of English icon Keegan

children.content: Former Liverpool and Newcastle United star Kevin Keegan is remembered as one of English football's first rock stars after his death at 75.

items.title: 'Our friends': How a nationwide Bug Hunt galvanized citizen scientists

children.content: More than 1 million observations of insects, spiders, snails and other invertebrates have been recorded by citizen scientists as part of Bug Hunt Australia since October 2025.

gallery.altText: Woman in broad-brimmed hat with a small black beetle on her finger.
A fly up close on a white and green flower.
Isabel and mum erica stand outdoors they are citizen scientists who often take part in the bug hunt
